title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
summary: Peter Sarlin launches QuTwo, a startup building infrastructure for enterprises to transition to quantum computing. This move prepares companies for the eventual shift to quantum computing, enabling a smoother transition.

title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
summary: Truecaller introduces a feature allowing one family member to receive alerts about scam calls and hang up on behalf of others. This feature matters as it helps protect vulnerable family members from scams.

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
summary:
Researchers introduce DIVE, a method to synthesize diverse tasks for AI tool use, improving generalizability. DIVE achieves this by executing real-world tools first and then deriving tasks from their traces. 
This matters because it addresses a major challenge in AI tool use, enabling more robust and versatile applications.

title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
summary: Researchers identify reasoning as a key bottleneck in autonomous driving, surpassing perception limitations. Integrating large language and multimodal models into AD systems could enable genuine comprehension, but a systematic framework is lacking.

title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
summary: Researchers introduce PACED, a framework that optimizes large language model (LLM) distillation by focusing on the student model's zone of proximal development. This approach reduces computational waste by concentrating on the student's competence frontier.

title: Measuring AI Agents' Progress on Multi-Step Cyber Attack Scenarios
summary: Researchers evaluate AI models on multi-step cyber attack scenarios, finding performance scales with compute budget and successive models outperform predecessors. This matters as it highlights AI's increasing potential for autonomous cyber threats.

title: Reversible Lifelong Model Editing via Semantic Routing-Based LoRA
summary: Researchers propose SoLA, a framework for editing Large Language Models through semantic routing-based LoRA modules. This approach avoids semantic drift and knowledge forgetting by dynamically activating modules via semantic matching.

title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang will deliver a keynote at the GPU Technology Conference (GTC) 2026. He will discuss Nvidia's vision for the future of computing and AI.

title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
summary: Rox AI, a sales automation startup, has reportedly reached a $1.2B valuation. This valuation comes just a year after its founding in 2024.

title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
summary: US military may use AI chatbots to rank targets and make strike recommendations, vetted by humans. This could automate targeting decisions, potentially increasing efficiency and speed in combat operations.

title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
summary: Meta AI can now automatically draft replies to buyer inquiries on Facebook Marketplace using listing information. This feature streamlines communication and saves sellers time, making it easier to manage transactions.

title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
summary: Tinder introduces in-person events and virtual speed dating to revive user base. This revamp aims to attract younger daters and reengage existing users.

title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
summary: Atlassian laid off 1,600 employees (10% of its workforce) to redirect funds to AI development. This move mirrors Block's recent staff cuts, prioritizing AI investment.

title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
summary: Bumble introduces AI-powered "Bee" to match users based on compatibility and goals. This new feature will shift the app's focus beyond swiping, enabling more personalized matches.

title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
summary: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for allegedly using her work without consent. This matters as it raises concerns about AI-powered tools exploiting authors' work without permission.

title: Exploratory Data Analysis for Credit Scoring with Python
summary:
Understanding default risk through statistical analysis of borrower and loan characteristics.

summary:

summary:
Navigating the performance cliff: How pairing MRL with int8 and binary quantization balances infrastructure costs with retrieval accuracy.
The post Scaling Vector Search: Comparing Quantization and Matryoshka Embeddings for 80% Cost Reduction appeared first on Towards Data…

title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
summary: Engineers are increasingly using AI to enhance and streamline product design in everyday items like cars, appliances, and medical devices. This matters because it can improve product efficiency, safety, and functionality.

title: I Finally Built My First AI App (And It Wasn’t What I Expected)
summary:
A beginner-friendly walkthrough of API calls, environment variables, and real-world AI infrastructure
The post I Finally Built My First AI App (And It Wasn’t What I Expected) appeared first on Towards Data Science.

title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
summary: Researchers propose a system to automate data product improvement using AI agents in a continuous optimization loop. This system enables domain experts to refine data products with human oversight, balancing automation and trust.

title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
summary: Researchers propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory for GUI agents that combines symbolic and continuous representations. This innovation enables GUI agents to learn from and adapt to complex workflows and interfaces.

title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
summary: Researchers propose Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning (HEAL), a framework to distill reasoning capabilities from large models into smaller ones. HEAL addresses the "Teacher Ceiling" limitation by actively intervening in the learning process.

title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
summary: Researchers introduce TRACED, a framework to assess LLM reasoning quality through geometric kinematics, decomposing reasoning into Progress and Stability. This approach improves performance and robustness across benchmarks by identifying correct reasoning patterns.

title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
summary: Researchers propose new prompt-based uncertainty elicitation techniques for large language models using imprecise probabilities. This addresses limitations in classical probabilistic uncertainty frameworks, particularly in ambiguous question-answering and self-reflection settings.

title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
Autonomous context compression
summary:
New: Autonomous context compression tool added to Deep Agents SDK, allowing models to compress their own context windows. 
This matters because it enables more efficient use of working memory, potentially improving model performance and decision-making.

title: An Intuitive Guide to MCMC (Part I): The Metropolis-Hastings Algorithm
summary:

title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
summary: Rakuten now uses Codex from OpenAI to fix software issues twice as fast. This is achieved through 50% reduced Mean Time To Resolve (MTTR) and automation of CI/CD reviews.

title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
summary:
New: A 27-year-old software engineer in Beijing is capitalizing on China's OpenClaw AI craze by launching his own company. 
It matters: This trend highlights the rapid adoption and monetization of AI tools in China.

title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
summary: Researchers design AI agents to resist prompt injection and social engineering by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data. This matters as it enhances AI security, preventing potential misuse and data breaches.

title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
summary: OpenAI has developed an agent runtime using its Responses API, allowing for secure and scalable execution of agents with files and state. This matters as it enables more complex AI interactions and applications.

title: Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI
summary: Wayfair leverages OpenAI models to automate ticket triage and enhance product attributes. This matters as it improves ecommerce support speed and catalog accuracy, enhancing the overall customer experience.

title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
summary: Researchers define "harness" as a crucial component of AI systems, built around models to make their intelligence useful. This concept matters as it outlines the necessary infrastructure to effectively deploy AI models in real-world applications.

title: How Coding Agents Are Reshaping Engineering, Product and Design
summary: EPD (Engineering, Product, and Design) at software company is about creating good software. Separate roles exist, but the end goal is functional software that solves a business problem that users can use. At the end of the day, this is just code. It is important to recognize…

title: Building a strong data infrastructure for AI agent success
summary: Companies are rapidly deploying AI agents, with 88% using AI in at least one business function. This growth indicates a significant shift towards AI adoption.

title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
summary: Niantic's AR expertise, developed for Pokémon Go, is being applied to delivery robots for precise navigation. This technology enables robots to accurately detect and avoid obstacles in real-time.

title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
summary:
Researchers introduce IH-Challenge, a training method for frontier LLMs to prioritize trusted instructions. 
This improves instruction hierarchy, safety, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
summary: AI-powered conflict tracking dashboards are being shared online, allowing users to monitor the Iran conflict in real-time. This matters because it reveals the increasing accessibility and transparency of military intelligence, potentially influencing public perception and global understanding of the conflict.

title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform, secured $100 million in Series B funding to develop AI-native infrastructure. This investment values Railway as a significant infrastructure startup, capitalizing on developer frustration with traditional platforms like AWS and Google Cloud.

title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
summary: Goose, a free open-source AI agent, offers similar functionality to Claude Code without subscription fees. This matters because it provides an affordable alternative to expensive AI coding tools, giving developers more control over their data.

title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
summary: Listen Labs, a startup, secured $69M in funding after a viral billboard campaign used AI tokens to attract engineers. The campaign's success showcases innovative hiring strategies in a competitive market.

title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
summary: Salesforce has released a rebuilt Slackbot AI agent capable of searching data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf of employees. This move positions Slack at the center of the "agentic AI" movement, where software agents work alongside humans to complete complex tasks, bolstering Salesforce's AI offerings.

title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
summary: Anthropic released Cowork, a new AI agent capability that allows non-technical users to complete tasks in their files without coding. This marks a significant inflection point in the AI market, positioning Anthropic to compete with major players in conversational and productivity AI.
